NKJV.1: Though I speak with the tongues of men and of angels, but have not love, I have become sounding brass or a clanging cymbal.
NKJV.2: And though I have the gift of prophecy, and understand all mysteries and all knowledge, and though I have all faith, so that I could remove mountains, but have not love, I am nothing.
NKJV.3: And though I bestow all my goods to feed the poor, and though I give my body to be burned, but have not love, it profits me nothing.
NKJV.4: Love suffers long and is kind; love does not envy; love does not parade itself, is not puffed up;
NKJV.5: does not behave rudely, does not seek its own, is not provoked, thinks no evil;
NKJV.6: does not rejoice in iniquity, but rejoices in the truth;
NKJV.7: bears all things, believes all things, hopes all things, endures all things.
NKJV.8: Love never fails. But whether there are prophecies, they will fail; whether there are tongues, they will cease; whether there is knowledge, it will vanish away.
NKJV.9: For we know in part and we prophesy in part.
NKJV.10: But when that which is perfect has come, then that which is in part will be done away.
NKJV.11: When I was a child, I spoke as a child, I understood as a child, I thought as a child; but when I became a man, I put away childish things.
NKJV.12: For now we see in a mirror, dimly, but then face to face. Now I know in part, but then I shall know just as I also am known.
NKJV.13: And now abide faith, hope, love, these three; but the greatest of these is love.

ESV.1: If I speak in the tongues of men and of angels, but have not love, I am a noisy gong or a clanging cymbal.
ESV.2: And if I have prophetic powers, and understand all mysteries and all knowledge, and if I have all faith, so as to remove mountains, but have not love, I am nothing.
ESV.3: If I give away all I have, and if I deliver up my body to be burned,  but have not love, I gain nothing.
ESV.4: Love is patient and kind; love does not envy or boast; it is not arrogant
ESV.5: or rude. It does not insist on its own way; it is not irritable or resentful;
ESV.6: it does not rejoice at wrongdoing, but rejoices with the truth.
ESV.7: Love bears all things, believes all things, hopes all things, endures all things.
ESV.8: Love never ends. As for prophecies, they will pass away; as for tongues, they will cease; as for knowledge, it will pass away.
ESV.9: For we know in part and we prophesy in part,
ESV.10: but when the perfect comes, the partial will pass away.
ESV.11: When I was a child, I spoke like a child, I thought like a child, I reasoned like a child. When I became a man, I gave up childish ways.
ESV.12: For now we see in a mirror dimly, but then face to face. Now I know in part; then I shall know fully, even as I have been fully known.
ESV.13: So now faith, hope, and love abide, these three; but the greatest of these is love.

NASB.1: If I speak with the tongues of men and of angels, but do not have love, I have become a noisy gong or a clanging cymbal.
NASB.2: If I have [the gift of] prophecy, and know all mysteries and all knowledge; and if I have all faith, so as to remove mountains, but do not have love, I am nothing.
NASB.3: And if I give all my possessions to feed [the poor], and if I surrender my body to be burned, but do not have love, it profits me nothing.
NASB.4: Love is patient, love is kind [and] is not jealous; love does not brag [and] is not arrogant,
NASB.5: does not act unbecomingly; it does not seek its own, is not provoked, does not take into account a wrong [suffered],
NASB.6: does not rejoice in unrighteousness, but rejoices with the truth;
NASB.7: bears all things, believes all things, hopes all things, endures all things.
NASB.8: Love never fails; but if [there are gifts of] prophecy, they will be done away; if [there are] tongues, they will cease; if [there is] knowledge, it will be done away.
NASB.9: For we know in part and we prophesy in part;
NASB.10: but when the perfect comes, the partial will be done away.
NASB.11: When I was a child, I used to speak like a child, think like a child, reason like a child; when I became a man, I did away with childish things.
NASB.12: For now we see in a mirror dimly, but then face to face; now I know in part, but then I will know fully just as I also have been fully known.
NASB.13: But now faith, hope, love, abide these three; but the greatest of these is love.

NLT.1: If I could speak all the languages of earth and of angels, but didn’t love others, I would only be a noisy gong or a clanging cymbal.
NLT.2: If I had the gift of prophecy, and if I understood all of God’s secret plans and possessed all knowledge, and if I had such faith that I could move mountains, but didn’t love others, I would be nothing.
NLT.3: If I gave everything I have to the poor and even sacrificed my body, I could boast about it; but if I didn’t love others, I would have gained nothing.
NLT.4: Love is patient and kind. Love is not jealous or boastful or proud
NLT.5: or rude. It does not demand its own way. It is not irritable, and it keeps no record of being wronged.
NLT.6: It does not rejoice about injustice but rejoices whenever the truth wins out.
NLT.7: Love never gives up, never loses faith, is always hopeful, and endures through every circumstance.
NLT.8: Prophecy and speaking in unknown languages and special knowledge will become useless. But love will last forever!
NLT.9: Now our knowledge is partial and incomplete, and even the gift of prophecy reveals only part of the whole picture!
NLT.10: But when the time of perfection comes, these partial things will become useless.
NLT.11: When I was a child, I spoke and thought and reasoned as a child. But when I grew up, I put away childish things.
NLT.12: Now we see things imperfectly, like puzzling reflections in a mirror, but then we will see everything with perfect clarity. All that I know now is partial and incomplete, but then I will know everything completely, just as God now knows me completely.
NLT.13: Three things will last forever — faith, hope, and love — and the greatest of these is love.

WEB.1: If I speak with the languages of men and of angels, but don’t have love, I have become sounding brass, or a clanging cymbal.
WEB.2: If I have the gift of prophecy, and know all mysteries and all knowledge; and if I have all faith, so as to remove mountains, but don’t have love, I am nothing.
WEB.3: If I dole out all my goods to feed the poor, and if I give my body to be burned, but don’t have love, it profits me nothing.
WEB.4: Love is patient and is kind; love doesn’t envy. Love doesn’t brag, is not proud,
WEB.5: doesn’t behave itself inappropriately, doesn’t seek its own way, is not provoked, takes no account of evil;
WEB.6: doesn’t rejoice in unrighteousness, but rejoices with the truth;
WEB.7: bears all things, believes all things, hopes all things, endures all things.
WEB.8: Love never fails. But where there are prophecies, they will be done away with. Where there are various languages, they will cease. Where there is knowledge, it will be done away with.
WEB.9: For we know in part, and we prophesy in part;
WEB.10: but when that which is complete has come, then that which is partial will be done away with.
WEB.11: When I was a child, I spoke as a child, I felt as a child, I thought as a child. Now that I have become a man, I have put away childish things.
WEB.12: For now we see in a mirror, dimly, but then face to face. Now I know in part, but then I will know fully, even as I was also fully known.
WEB.13: But now faith, hope, and love remain — these three. The greatest of these is love.

CSB17.1: If I speak human or angelic tongues <sup>[1]</sup> but do not have love, <sup>ⓗ</sup> I am a noisy gong <sup>ⓘ</sup> or a clanging cymbal.
CSB17.2: If I have the gift of prophecy <sup>ⓙ</sup> and understand all mysteries and all knowledge, and if I have all faith so that I can move mountains <sup>ⓚ</sup> but do not have love, I am nothing.
CSB17.3: And if I give away all my possessions, and if I give over my body in order to boast <sup>[2]</sup> but do not have love, I gain nothing.
CSB17.4: Love is patient, <sup>ⓛ</sup> love is kind. Love does not envy, <sup>ⓜ</sup> is not boastful, is not arrogant, <sup>ⓝ</sup>
CSB17.5: is not rude, is not self-seeking, <sup>ⓐ</sup> is not irritable, <sup>ⓑ</sup> and does not keep a record of wrongs.
CSB17.6: Love finds no joy in unrighteousness but rejoices in the truth. <sup>ⓒ</sup>
CSB17.7: It bears all things, believes all things, hopes all things, endures <sup>ⓓ</sup> all things.
CSB17.8: Love never ends. <sup>ⓔ</sup> But as for prophecies, they will come to an end; as for tongues, they will cease; as for knowledge, it will come to an end.
CSB17.9: For we know in part, and we prophesy in part,
CSB17.10: but when the perfect comes, the partial will come to an end.
CSB17.11: When I was a child, I spoke like a child, I thought like a child, I reasoned like a child. When I became a man, I put aside childish things.
CSB17.12: For now we see only a reflection <sup>[3]</sup> as in a mirror, but then face to face. <sup>ⓕ</sup> Now I know in part, but then I will know fully, <sup>ⓖ</sup> as I am fully known. <sup>ⓗ</sup>
CSB17.13: Now these three remain: faith, hope, <sup>ⓘ</sup> and love — but the greatest of these is love.

AMP.1: If I speak with the tongues of men and of angels, but have not love [for others growing out of God's love for me], then I have become only a noisy gong or a clanging cymbal [just an annoying distraction].
AMP.2: And if I have the gift of prophecy [and speak a new message from God to the people], and understand all mysteries, and [possess] all knowledge; and if I have all [sufficient] faith so that I can remove mountains, but do not have love [reaching out to others], I am nothing.
AMP.3: If I give all my possessions to feed the poor, and if I surrender my body to be burned, but do not have love, it does me no good at all.
AMP.4: Love endures with patience and serenity, love is kind and thoughtful, and is not jealous or envious; love does not brag and is not proud or arrogant.
AMP.5: It is not rude; it is not self-seeking, it is not provoked [nor overly sensitive and easily angered]; it does not take into account a wrong endured.
AMP.6: It does not rejoice at injustice, but rejoices with the truth [when right and truth prevail].
AMP.7: Love bears all things [regardless of what comes], believes all things [looking for the best in each one], hopes all things [remaining steadfast during difficult times], endures all things [without weakening].
AMP.8: Love never fails [it never fades nor ends]. But as for prophecies, they will pass away; as for tongues, they will cease; as for the gift of special knowledge, it will pass away.
AMP.9: For we know in part, and we prophesy in part [for our knowledge is fragmentary and incomplete].
AMP.10: But when that which is complete and perfect comes, that which is incomplete and partial will pass away.
AMP.11: When I was a child, I talked like a child, I thought like a child, I reasoned like a child; when I became a man, I did away with childish things.
AMP.12: For now [in this time of imperfection] we see in a mirror dimly [a blurred reflection, a riddle, an enigma], but then [when the time of perfection comes we will see reality] face to face. Now I know in part [just in fragments], but then I will know fully, just as I have been fully known [by God].
AMP.13: And now there remain: faith [abiding trust in God and His promises], hope [confident expectation of eternal salvation], love [unselfish love for others growing out of God's love for me], these three [the choicest graces]; but the greatest of these is love.

MSG.1: If I speak with human eloquence and angelic ecstasy but don't love, I'm nothing but the creaking of a rusty gate.
MSG.2: If I speak God's Word with power, revealing all his mysteries and making everything plain as day, and if I have faith that says to a mountain, "Jump," and it jumps, but I don't love, I'm nothing.
MSG.3: If I give everything I own to the poor and even go to the stake to be burned as a martyr, but I don't love, I've gotten nowhere. So, no matter what I say, what I believe, and what I do, I'm bankrupt without love.
MSG.4: Love never gives up. Love cares more for others than for self. Love doesn't want what it doesn't have. Love doesn't strut, Doesn't have a swelled head,
MSG.5: Doesn't force itself on others, Isn't always "me first," Doesn't fly off the handle, Doesn't keep score of the sins of others,
MSG.6: Doesn't revel when others grovel, Takes pleasure in the flowering of truth,
MSG.7: Puts up with anything, Trusts God always, Always looks for the best, Never looks back, But keeps going to the end.
MSG.8: Love never dies. Inspired speech will be over some day; praying in tongues will end; understanding will reach its limit.
MSG.9: We know only a portion of the truth, and what we say about God is always incomplete.
MSG.10: But when the Complete arrives, our incompletes will be canceled.
MSG.11: When I was an infant at my mother's breast, I gurgled and cooed like any infant. When I grew up, I left those infant ways for good.
MSG.12: We don't yet see things clearly. We're squinting in a fog, peering through a mist. But it won't be long before the weather clears and the sun shines bright! We'll see it all then, see it all as clearly as God sees us, knowing him directly just as he knows us!
MSG.13: But for right now, until that completeness, we have three things to do to lead us toward that consummation: Trust steadily in God, hope unswervingly, love extravagantly. And the best of the three is love.

NIV.1: If I speak in the tongues of men and of angels, but have not love, I am only a resounding gong or a clanging cymbal.
NIV.2: If I have the gift of prophecy and can fathom all mysteries and all knowledge, and if I have a faith that can move mountains, but have not love, I am nothing.
NIV.3: If I give all I possess to the poor and surrender my body to the flames, but have not love, I gain nothing.
NIV.4: Love is patient, love is kind. It does not envy, it does not boast, it is not proud.
NIV.5: It is not rude, it is not self-seeking, it is not easily angered, it keeps no record of wrongs.
NIV.6: Love does not delight in evil but rejoices with the truth.
NIV.7: It always protects, always trusts, always hopes, always perseveres.
NIV.8: Love never fails. But where there are prophecies, they will cease; where there are tongues, they will be stilled; where there is knowledge, it will pass away.
NIV.9: For we know in part and we prophesy in part,
NIV.10: but when perfection comes, the imperfect disappears.
NIV.11: When I was a child, I talked like a child, I thought like a child, I reasoned like a child. When I became a man, I put childish ways behind me.
NIV.12: Now we see but a poor reflection as in a mirror; then we shall see face to face. Now I know in part; then I shall know fully, even as I am fully known.
NIV.13: And now these three remain: faith, hope and love. But the greatest of these is love.

NRSVCE.1: If I speak in the tongues of mortals and of angels, but do not have love, I am a noisy gong or a clanging cymbal.
NRSVCE.2: And if I have prophetic powers, and understand all mysteries and all knowledge, and if I have all faith, so as to remove mountains, but do not have love, I am nothing.
NRSVCE.3: If I give away all my possessions, and if I hand over my body so that I may boast, but do not have love, I gain nothing.
NRSVCE.4: Love is patient; love is kind; love is not envious or boastful or arrogant
NRSVCE.5: or rude. It does not insist on its own way; it is not irritable or resentful;
NRSVCE.6: it does not rejoice in wrongdoing, but rejoices in the truth.
NRSVCE.7: It bears all things, believes all things, hopes all things, endures all things.
NRSVCE.8: Love never ends. But as for prophecies, they will come to an end; as for tongues, they will cease; as for knowledge, it will come to an end.
NRSVCE.9: For we know only in part, and we prophesy only in part;
NRSVCE.10: but when the complete comes, the partial will come to an end.
NRSVCE.11: When I was a child, I spoke like a child, I thought like a child, I reasoned like a child; when I became an adult, I put an end to childish ways.
NRSVCE.12: For now we see in a mirror, dimly, but then we will see face to face. Now I know only in part; then I will know fully, even as I have been fully known.
NRSVCE.13: And now faith, hope, and love abide, these three; and the greatest of these is love.

NET.1: If I speak in the tongues of men and of angels, but I do not have love, I am a noisy gong or a clanging cymbal.
NET.2: And if I have prophecy, and know all mysteries and all knowledge, and if I have all faith so that I can remove mountains, but do not have love, I am nothing.
NET.3: If I give away everything I own, and if I give over my body in order to boast, but do not have love, I receive no benefit.
NET.4: Love is patient, love is kind, it is not envious. Love does not brag, it is not puffed up.
NET.5: It is not rude, it is not self-serving, it is not easily angered or resentful.
NET.6: It is not glad about injustice, but rejoices in the truth.
NET.7: It bears all things, believes all things, hopes all things, endures all things.
NET.8: Love never ends. But if there are prophecies, they will be set aside; if there are tongues, they will cease; if there is knowledge, it will be set aside.
NET.9: For we know in part, and we prophesy in part,
NET.10: but when what is perfect comes, the partial will be set aside.
NET.11: When I was a child, I talked like a child, I thought like a child, I reasoned like a child. But when I became an adult, I set aside childish ways.
NET.12: For now we see in a mirror indirectly, but then we will see face to face. Now I know in part, but then I will know fully, just as I have been fully known.
NET.13: And now these three remain: faith, hope, and love. But the greatest of these is love.
